$schema_note: Slack Block Kit payloads for the take-home. Each entry is one message a bot posts to a channel, with a `blocks` array — the exact shape Slack's Block Kit uses. Your job is to render these faithfully (see README). Block types present in this dataset: `header` (plain_text title), `context` (array of `mrkdwn`/`image` elements, small + muted), `section` (a `text` mrkdwn body AND/OR a `fields` array of 2-col mrkdwn), `divider`, `actions` (array of interactive `elements`). Interactive elements: `button` (has `text`, optional `url`, optional `style`: 'primary' | 'danger' | default, and an `action_id`), and `static_select` (a `placeholder` + `options[]`, each with `text` + `value`, and an `action_id`). `mrkdwn` is Slack's markdown dialect: *bold*, _italic_, `code`, code fences, <url|label> links, :emoji:, and \n newlines — you must parse a sensible subset. Each message also carries app-level metadata (`scenario`, `channel`, `postedAt`, `traceId`, `lifecycle`) OUTSIDE the blocks array; blocks is the part Slack would actually render. NOTE: the facts in each card (failing span, error, model, cost, severity, feedback) are consistent with the referenced trace in traces.json — the bot's added analysis (root cause, proposed fix, PR) is extra context a real triage bot would attach.

messages.scenario: A trace failed when a tool call was refused; the monitor bot posts an alert.

text.text: A staging run hit a `ToolException: connection refused` while calling the `lookup_order` tool, and never completed. The model had already decided to look up the order.

messages.scenario: Bot acknowledges and starts investigating, posted as a threaded reply.

elements.text: :eyes: *Investigating* — I'll reply here when I have a root cause.

messages.scenario: Investigation finished with a root cause and a proposed fix. The richest card — long body, a code/log block, a 2-col field section, and a full action row with a select.

text.text:
*Root cause*
The agent calls the `lookup_order` tool, which reaches the orders service over HTTP. The service refused the connection (`ToolException: connection refused`) and the agent has no retry or fallback, so the run aborts. ~13% of `customer_support_agent` runs in the last hour hit the same path.

messages.scenario: A human merged the fix; the card collapses to a terminal resolved state.

text.text: Retry + timeout handling added to the `lookup_order` call. Error rate on this path dropped to *0%* over the last 12 minutes.

messages.scenario: A different failure — a RAG run's generation step failed after retrieval succeeded. Shorter alert, no customer field.

text.text: :warning: Retrieval succeeded, but the final `ChatOpenAI` generation failed with `ToolException: connection refused`. The run produced no answer.

messages.scenario: Not a failure — a successful run got a thumbs-down with a comment. Tests rendering a card driven by feedback rather than an error, including a quoted user comment.

text.text:
> Hallucinated a detail

Reviewer flagged the response as inaccurate. Worth checking grounding before this reaches customers.
